channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Oil Rockets Higher as Trump Declares Iran Ceasefire Over
item.description: 'To me, I think it's over', Trump said during a press meeting in Ankara.

item.title: Oil hits two-week high after Trump says Iran peace deal is over
item.description: Oil prices rose more than 5% on Wednesday, 8 July, hitting a two-week high after US President Donald Trump said the peace deal with Iran was over, reviving fears of supply disruptions in the Middle East.

item.title: MidOcean Energy secures $1.1bn backing from UAE royal family
item.description: The Private Department of Abu Dhabi’s Crown Prince Sheikh Mohammed bin Khalid Al Nahyan has announced an investment of $1.13bn in MidOcean Energy.

item.title: Oil and gas industry witnessing a transformation with Industrial Internet
item.description: GlobalData forecasts that the global Industrial Internet market will reach $553bn in revenue by 2029, up from $259bn in 2024.

item.title: Eni Starts Building Italian Battery Hub, Agrees Chilean Lithium Investment
item.description: Eni broke ground for a project to produce lithium-iron-phosphate batteries at the Brindisi industrial site in Italy, as well as announced an agreement to acquire a stake in a Chilean lithium project.

item.title: Equinor Awards Contracts for Norway Tiebacks
item.description: Equinor and its partners signed contracts worth around $613 million for four tieback projects in different stages of development offshore Norway.

item.title: Equinor awards $612m of contracts for four NCS projects
item.description: Equinor, along with its partners, has awarded contracts amounting to approximately Nkr6bn ($612.12m) for four subsea development projects on the Norwegian Continental Shelf (NCS).

item.title: US Cancels Oil Waiver and Strikes Iran Anew
item.description: The U.S. carried out a new round of strikes in Iran targeting more than 80 sites and revoked a waiver allowing new sales of its oil, further imperiling a peace agreement after a series of attacks on ships in the Strait…

item.title: 63 Million Barrels Of Crude Stuck At Sea As U.S. Pulls Iran Sanction Waiver
item.description: Iran was struggling to sell its crude to buyers in Asia outside China even before the U.S. rescinded overnight the sanctions waiver that allowed Tehran to sell its oil without penalties. The Iranian attacks on three…

item.title: Oil Prices Surge 6% Even as Tankers Push Through Hormuz
item.description: Thin tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz continued early on Wednesday despite the fresh flare-up in the Middle East, but more vessels appear to be reconsidering moving toward the chokepoint after the U.S. and…

item.title: Bahrain and Kuwait Restore Power After Iranian Attacks
item.description: Kuwait and Bahrain have started to restore power to residential areas following Iranian strikes on the two Gulf nations in retaliation for U.S. strikes on Iran in the latest conflagration in the Middle East that…

item.title: Oil and LNG Tankers Make U-Turns After Fresh Hormuz Escalation
item.description: The renewed hostilities and attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz again put an abrupt end to the steady flow of oil and gas tankers moving in and out of the Persian Gulf. At least four oil and LNG tankers have made…

item.title: China Lifts Fuel Export Curbs as Refiners Ramp Up Overseas Shipments
item.description: China has lifted restrictions on the exports of refined fuels, allowing state-owned refiners to resume shipments overseas this month, as well as one private refiner, Reuters reported today, citing unnamed sources. For…

item.title: Oil Prices Jump After Iran Attacks Commercial Vessels
item.description: Crude oil prices moved higher today, extending gains from earlier this week after the United States and Iran exchanged military strikes again, following Iran’s attacks on three vessels off the Omani coast. The United…

item.title: Fed President Stays Optimistic as Energy Prices Fall
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said he expects falling energy prices to drive a drop in overall inflation over the next few months.

item.title: Oil Jumps on Hormuz Shipping Attacks
item.description: The US revoked a waiver allowing Iranian oil sales following shipping attacks that threatened the fragile ceasefire with Iran.

item.title: Why the AI Boom Is About to Break the U.S. Power Grid
item.description: The American power grid is about to fail the most important industry it has ever been asked to support. In fact…the dominoes in this potentially disastrous scenario have already begun to fall. Large companies that right…

item.title: The Next Oil Price Spike Could Come Sooner Than Traders Think
item.description: Oil prices have dropped back to pre-war levels since the United States and Iran agreed to negotiate a deal under a framework that included the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. With oil flows out of the Middle East…

item.title: Has Iraq Finally Run Out of Road in Its Double-Dealings with the West?
item.description: Late June/early July normally brings twin events from Iraq for the global oil industry: an announcement that it intends to increase its crude oil production to either 6 or 7 million barrels per day (bpd) within three…

item.title: Trump's Nuclear Loan Program Won't Fix What's Really Broken
item.description: Donald Trump is trying to push forward a nuclear power renaissance in the United States as the AI boom presents the country with a major looming energy deficit. The federal government recently announced over $17 billion…
